'''Willie Poching''' is a professional [[rugby league]] football coach and former rugby league footballer. He is the former head-coach of [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the [[Super League]]. When he was appointed head coach of Wakefield, he became the first Samoan head coach of a first grade rugby league club.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Ewart |first1=Richard |title=Willie Poching first Samoan head coach of first grade rugby league club |url=https://www.abc.net.au/radio-australia/programs/pacificbeat/samoa-rugby-poching/13605180 |website=ABC Radio Australia |date=27 October 2021 |access-date=1 May 2022}}</ref> A former [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]], and Samoa international representative forward, he spent his career playing for clubs in New Zealand, Australia and finally England, winning the Super League championship with Leeds Rhinos in 2004.<ref name="stats1"/>

==Playing career==
===New Zealand===
Poching played for the [[Northcote Tigers]], [[Marist Saints]] and [[Mount Albert Lions]] in the [[Auckland Rugby League]] competition.<ref>Richard Becht. ''A New Breed Rising: The Warriors Winfield Cup Challenge''. Auckland, HarperCollins, 1994. {{ISBN|1-86950-154-3}}. p.183</ref> He made the [[Junior Kiwis]] in 1991 and captained the side in 1992. He played in 4 games for [[Auckland rugby league team|Auckland]] between 1992 and 1993, before signing a junior contract with the [[Brisbane Broncos]] for 1994. During that year he toured New Zealand with Western Samoa and captained them in a game against a [[Manawatu rugby league team|Manawatu XIII]].<ref name=Samoa>''Lion Red Rugby League Annual 1994'', [[New Zealand Rugby Football League]], 1994. p.50</ref> Poching represented the [[Junior Kiwis]] in 1991 and 1992, captaining the side in 1992.

He joined the [[New Zealand Warriors|Auckland Warriors]] in 1995, however he only played two games for the club and was not re-signed for the 1996 season. Poching played for [[Samoa national rugby league team|Western Samoa]] at the [[1995 Rugby League World Cup|1995 World Cup]], and also captained Samoa during their [[2000 Rugby League World Cup|2000 World Cup]].

===England===
Over his long career Poching played for [[Wakefield Trinity]] ([[Captain (sports)|captain]]) and was a huge crowd favourite and club captain for his final season. He then joined the [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] club in the [[Super League]]. Poching played for the Leeds side from the interchange bench in their [[2004 Super League Grand Final]] victory against the [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]] club. As [[Super League IX]] champions, the Leeds club faced [[2004 NRL season]] premiers, the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]] in the [[2005 World Club Challenge]]. Poching played from the interchange bench, scoring a [[Try (rugby)|try]] in Leeds 39-32 victory. Poching played for Leeds side in the [[2005 Challenge Cup]] Final from the interchange bench in their loss against [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]].<ref>{{cite news|title=Hull stun Leeds in Challenge Cup|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/challenge_cup/4169230.stm|access-date=17 August 2013|newspaper=[[BBC News]]|date=27 August 2005}}</ref> He played for [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] at {{rlp|sr}} in their [[2005 Super League Grand Final]] loss against [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]]. He played his single game for [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] against [[England national rugby league team|England]] at [[Halliwell Jones Stadium]], [[Warrington]] during the [[2005 Rugby League Tri-Nations|2005 Tri-Nations campaign]].  Poching was forced to retire due to injury at the completion of 2006 season.

==Coaching career==
Poching was the academy coach at Leeds as well as the head coach of [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]].<ref>[http://www.therhinos.co.uk/news/7999.php Poching names strong Samoan squad] ''therhinos.co.uk'', 6 October 2009</ref> He moved from Leeds to become assistant coach to [[Tony Smith (rugby league born 1967)|Tony Smith]] at the [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] club in the 2010 post season, and [[Coach (sports)|coach]]ed the team to win the [[Super League XVI|League Leader's Shield in 2011]], [[2012 Challenge Cup|Challenge Cup in 2012]], and to two Grand Final defeats in [[2012 Super League Grand Final|2012 (to Leeds)]] and [[2013 Super League Grand Final|2013 (to Wigan)]]. Poching signed as assistant coach at the [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] side for the 2017 season. He later re-joined [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] and [[Tony Smith (rugby league, born 1967)|Tony Smith]] as assistant coach before returning to [[Wakefield Trinity]] as an interim head coach.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/54138827 |title=Willie Poching: Wakefield to bring in their former second-row forward as assistant coach |publisher=BBC Sport |date=13 September 2020 |accessdate=16 March 2021}}</ref> Poching was named head coach on 22 September 2021 after a successful interim stint.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Wakefield appoint Poching as head coach|language=en-GB|work=BBC Sport|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/58651578|access-date=2021-09-23}}</ref>
On 12 September 2022, Poching announced he was departing Wakefield Trinity.  The club finished 10th on the table and narrowly avoided relegation to the [[RFL Championship]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/12695794/super-league-willie-poching-departs-as-wakefield-trinity-head-coach/|title=Super League: Willie Poching departs as Wakefield Trinity head coach|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>

==Personal life==
Poching father Eddie was the first manager of the [[Samoa national rugby league team]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Rugby League Samoa – Our History |url=https://www.rugbyleaguesamoa.com/rls-our-history/ |website=Rugby League Samoa |access-date=1 May 2022}}</ref>

Poching gave son Kobe Poching, his Super League debut in the Boxing Day 2021 clash between Wakefield and Leeds.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Willie Poching recalls 'emotional' day when giving son Kobe Wakefield Trinity senior debut|language=en-GB|author=Dave Craven|publisher=The Yorkshire Post|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/poching-on-emotional-day-when-giving-son-kobe-senior-trinity-debut-3519838|access-date=2022-04-30}}</ref>
